refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall maintain teleoperation communication quality such that packet loss does not exceed the customer-defined limit under agreed network conditions.
refined_fr.rationale: Refining the original FR clarifies the performance metric (packet loss) and the operational context (agreed network conditions), ensuring measurable and testable requirements.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure packet loss percentage during teleoperation sessions under controlled network conditions and verify it meets the customer-defined threshold.
sub_frs: FR1: Monitor network link quality to detect packet loss in real-time.
FR2: Implement error correction and packet retransmission mechanisms to reduce effective packet loss.
FR3: Provide feedback and alerts to teleoperation control systems when packet loss approaches limits.

candidate_dps: DP1: Real-time network quality monitoring module with packet loss detection algorithms.
DP2: Adaptive communication protocol with error correction and retransmission capabilities.
DP3: Teleoperation control interface integration for feedback and alerting.
selected_dp.selected_dp: Combination of DP1, DP2, and DP3 to comprehensively address packet loss monitoring, mitigation, and operator feedback.

potential_sources: DP1 and DP2 tightly coupled due to real-time data exchange.
DP3 depends on DP1 for accurate status updates.
coupling_check.notes: Coupling is expected and manageable within the teleoperation domain; interfaces are well-defined.

assumptions_risks: Assumes network conditions are within agreed parameters.
Risk of packet loss spikes beyond correction capability.
Assumes teleoperation control system can process alerts timely.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider modularizing error correction separately if complexity grows.

im_boundary.name: Teleoperation
im_boundary.scope: Software and communication modules responsible for teleoperation control, network monitoring, and communication quality management.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Bi-directional communication channel carrying teleoperation data subject to packet loss.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Interface to send network quality and packet loss status to NGHE Solution for monitoring.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Receives teleoperation commands and tasking instructions.

module_containment_scores.notes: High diffusion risk if teleoperation logic is spread here; low containment for teleoperation-specific functions.
Vehicle module is physical hardware focused; poor containment for teleoperation communication logic.
Best containment and lowest diffusion risk for teleoperation communication and packet loss management.
Safety Services focus on alerts and safety policies; not suitable for teleoperation communication logic.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Centralizing teleoperation communication quality management within the Teleoperation module minimizes diffusion and improves maintainability.
containment_risks: Potential overload of Teleoperation module if additional unrelated functions are added.
Dependency on Network Link interface reliability.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Monitor module size and consider splitting communication monitoring and control if complexity increases.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The selected DPs comprehensively address the refined FR sub-functions with clear mapping and manageable coupling.

diffusion_risks: Minimal diffusion risk by assigning all teleoperation communication functions to the Teleoperation module.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Containment scores strongly favor Teleoperation module, supporting architectural clarity.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification can be performed via telemetry data and network link monitoring within the Teleoperation module.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of FR to DP and IM with well-defined interfaces.

recommended_revisions: Review potential overload of Teleoperation module in future decompositions.
Validate network link interface robustness under varying conditions.

decision.justification: Current decomposition level sufficiently refines the FR, selects appropriate DPs, and assigns IM boundaries with clear interfaces and manageable risks.
next_level_sub_frs: FR1: Monitor network link quality to detect packet loss in real-time.
FR2: Implement error correction and packet retransmission mechanisms to reduce effective packet loss.
FR3: Provide feedback and alerts to teleoperation control systems when packet loss approaches limits.

revise.why: No immediate revisions required; decomposition is consistent and complete at this level.

assumptions: Network conditions remain within agreed parameters.
Teleoperation control system can process alerts timely.
open_risks: Packet loss spikes beyond correction capability.
Potential module overload if Teleoperation module scope expands.
evidence_needed: Telemetry data demonstrating packet loss under operational conditions.
Performance metrics of error correction and retransmission mechanisms.
